chunks.text: # About Prashant ## Background Prashant is an MBA student with a background spanning physics, analytics, and business strategy. He's aspiring toward two paths: product management, and exploring risk finance as a potential direction — the latter still an open exploration rather than a settled choice. ## Coordinator of the Creative Club Prashant is the Coordinator of the Creative Club. This is also the reason Ari exists in this form — a crab chatbot on a portfolio site is itself a small proof of a belief he holds: that creativity and professionalism aren't in tension. You can be playful and rigorous at the same time. ## Technical credibility He has hands-on experience building data-driven applications, which gives him technical credibility when working alongside engineers rather than just directing them from a distance.

# Achievements ## Finquest — 1st Runner-up Prashant's team was the 1st runner-up in Finquest, a finance-based quiz competition conducted by IIM Rohtak. ## Top 20 out of 5,000+ teams — FinSpark'26 His team was shortlisted among the Top 20 finalist teams out of a pool of over 5,000 teams for FinSpark'26 — India's Banking Cybersecurity Innovation Hackathon, presented by Bank of Maharashtra in association with the Indian Banks' Association, the Department of Financial Services (Government of India), and COEP Technological University.

# How Ari Works — The Chatbot Itself Ari is a RAG (Retrieval Augmented Generation) chatbot built on: - Cloudflare Workers (serverless, free tier — 100k requests/day) - NVIDIA NIM API (DeepSeek V4 Pro for chat generation) - Workers AI (embedding for vector search) - Cloudflare KV (session memory — persists across page refreshes, 6hr expiry) - Pre-computed knowledge base with cosine similarity search Flow: 1. User opens chat → POST /greet checks KV for returning visitor 2. New visitor → asked "what brings you by?" with quick-reply chips (recruiter/builder/curious) 3. Visitor selects type → POST /classify saves it, returns personalized greeting with relevant highlight 4. User asks question → query embedded, compared against knowledge chunks via cosine similarity 5. Top matching chunks retrieved as context alongside chat history from KV 6. DeepSeek V4 Pro generates a grounded, personality-driven answer 7. Conversation saved to KV (last 10 messages, 6 hour TTL) Architecture: Static HTML widget on GitHub Pages → calls Cloudflare Worker → NVIDIA NIM API. Session memory in KV. Personality in persona.js. No backend server to manage.

# Experience ## Internship — building an AI agent product Prashant worked on building a product during an internship, focused on the full customer-experience side: how the product would be perceived by customers, how it should be positioned, and examining use cases from the customer's perspective — what it could be used for and by whom. (Specific details of the product itself aren't shareable — this is a description of the role and skills involved, not the product's internals.) ## Working with engineering teams As part of that internship, he presented a structured build format to the engineering team, covering the UI and how the AI agent should respond — translating product/customer thinking into something engineers could build against. ## Data architecture and privacy He also developed the database structure for that product, built around a redaction policy for customer data — designed so the agent could not tell which piece of data belonged to which specific person.

# Why hire Prashant ## No prior experience before this internship Prashant doesn't come in with years of engineering or product experience — this internship was his first real hands-on experience building a product. He comes from a non-technical background and doesn't have deep engineering chops, and isn't trying to present himself as if he does. ## What he brings instead: visualization and product storytelling His actual strength is the ability to visualize a product before it's built — to see the workable story behind it: why this feature and not that one, why this direction and not another. That's the core of what he brings to the table: turning a vague idea into a structure someone else (an engineer, a team) can actually build against. ## Proof, not just claim His portfolio, Ari (this chatbot), and his other projects are the evidence for this — things he built without a formal engineering background, learning by doing and evolving through hands-on iteration each time. He has a knack for trying new things and getting better at them as he goes, not for arriving already expert. ## What excites him Challenges excite him. Problems intrigue him. His running motto: "any problem is meant to be solved — it's just a question of when." ## A real weakness: persistence He's very persistent with problems that grab him. Letting something go isn't his forte — at least not until he's reached a genuine conclusion, not a surface-level one. It's a double-edged trait: it means real follow-through, but it also means he can hold onto something longer than might be efficient, if he hasn't yet felt satisfied that the problem is actually solved.

# Projects — What Prashant Has Built OCIE — Guideline ↔ Pipeline Mapper (Next.js, Postgres, NLP) - NSCLC drug-to-guideline mapping by biomarker and line of therapy - Dashboard with NLP-tagged evidence from guideline text Onco-Directory (Next.js, Supabase, Clerk, TypeScript) - Verified professional network for oncology - Trust tied to medical license IDs — no spam - Graph-based connection engine by specialisation - Direct messaging for referrals and consultations OncoAssist — Clinical Analytics Dashboard (Next.js, Recharts, Supabase) - Competitive intelligence platform for pharma strategy teams - Maps drug trial trajectories — completed, active, new initiations - Surfaces cancer targets and trial structure AI Hiring Signals Pipeline (Python, BERTopic, Granger Causality) - Job posting tracking across frontier AI labs - BERTopic + Granger causality to predict product announcements Political Discourse Topic Modeling (LDA, scikit-learn, Python) - LDA-based pipeline analyzing political speeches - Interactive dashboard with word clouds and topic weights

# Skills — What Prashant Brings to the Table ## Product thinking and product storytelling His strongest skill. The ability to visualize a product before it's built — to see the workable story behind it: why this feature and not that one, why this direction and not another. Turning a vague idea into a structured build that engineers can actually build against. ## Data architecture and system design Designing database schemas and data pipelines from scratch. Understanding how data flows through a system and structuring it for privacy, scalability, and clarity. Built the database structure for an AI agent product with a redaction policy for customer data. ## Translating between business and engineering Speaking both languages. Can take a product/customer question and translate it into a structured build format for engineering teams — defining UI behavior, agent responses, and data requirements in terms developers can execute against. ## Analytics and visualization Building dashboards and analytical products that surface actionable insights. Experience with Recharts, ECharts, Tableau. Designing KPIs, cohort analysis, and funnel analysis. Turning raw data into strategic output. ## Machine learning and NLP Practical experience with XGBoost, Random Forest, scikit-learn, BERTopic, LDA, scispaCy, and NER. Built an AI hiring signals pipeline using BERTopic and Granger causality. Built a political discourse topic modeling pipeline. ## Technical stack Python, SQL, Postgres, Next.js, TypeScript, Supabase, Vercel. Cloudflare Workers and serverless architecture. Building full-stack data products end to end. ## First-principles reasoning Physics background gives him a foundation in mathematical modeling and breaking complex problems down from first principles. This shows up in how he approaches unfamiliar domains — learn the structure, then build from there. ## Creative and professional balance Coordinator of the Creative Club at BITS Pilani. Believes creativity and rigor aren't in tension — you can be playful and professional at

problems down from first principles. This shows up in how he approaches unfamiliar domains — learn the structure, then build from there. ## Creative and professional balance Coordinator of the Creative Club at BITS Pilani. Believes creativity and rigor aren't in tension — you can be playful and professional at the same time. Ari (a crab chatbot on a portfolio site) is itself proof of that belief.
